articles.title: Trump touts destruction in Iran, Ackman looks forward to a whopping victory dividend
articles.expand: Summary: Donald Trump claims major military success in Iran, but provides no verifiable detail on targets or impact. Bill Ackman frames the war as one-sided and bullish for equities, urging investors to “buy quality.” Both messages lean heavily on confidence and narrative, not confirmed data. History suggests early war claims are often overstated o...

articles.title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Breakdown below 0.6900 underpins bearish bias
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair is down 0.3% around 0.6850 during the Asian trade at the start of the week. The Aussie pair faces selling pressure as risk-off impulse, driven by fears of escalating conflicts in the Middle East, is acting as a key drag on the Australian Dollar (AUD).

articles.title: BoJ Warns of “Behind the Curve” Risk as Yen Depreciation Amplifies Inflation Pressure
articles.expand: Bank of Japan struck a more urgent tone in the Summary of Opinions from its March meeting, warning of the risk of “falling behind the curve” if policy tightening lags underlying inflation dynamics. With real interest rates still deeply negative and price pressures showing signs of persistence, policymakers acknowledged that delayed action could eve...

articles.title: Reserve Bank of India FX intervention move after hours on Friday. INR chaos day ahead?
articles.expand: The RBI’s after-hours announcement of FX position caps is being viewed as a form of indirect intervention to support the rupee. While the policy forces short covering and limits speculative pressure, banks warn that rapid implementation could trigger disorderly unwinds and near-term volatility. Summary: The Reserve Bank of India announced new FX po...

articles.title: Judgment Week: Fed’s Rate Deadlock and the Non-Farm Payroll Verdict
articles.expand: Global markets have entered a definitive “Judgment Week” for the Federal Reserve, where the future of the 2026 interest rate path will be tested against a backdrop of $110 oil. Following February’s shock loss of -92k jobs, the Friday NFP report serves as the final verdict on whether the U.S. labor market is merely cooling or officially […] Th...

articles.title: PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.9223 (expected was 6.9205)
articles.expand: China operates a managed floating exchange rate system, under which the renminbi (yuan) is allowed to trade within a prescribed band around a central reference rate, or midpoint, set each trading day by the PBOC. The current trading band permits the currency to move plus or minus 2% from the official midpoint during onshore trading hours. Each morn...

articles.title: Japanese Yen outperforms as BoJ’s Ueda expresses readiness to intervene in FX markets
articles.expand: The Japanese Yen (JPY) trades higher against its major currency peers during the Asian trading session on Monday, snapping a four-day losing streak against the US Dollar (USD), trading 0.2% down to near 160.00.

articles.title: USD/CHF sits near monthly peak as bulls await move beyond 0.8000 amid a firmer USD
articles.expand: The USD/CHF pair attracts fresh buyers following a modest Asian session dip to the 0.7970 area and touches a fresh high since January 19 at the start of a new week.

articles.title: Japan signals readiness for FX intervention. Yen weakened past 160, has since had a bounce
articles.expand: Japan has signalled a higher likelihood of FX intervention, with Mimura’s use of “decisive” marking a clear escalation in rhetoric. Combined with Ueda’s earlier comments, authorities are showing increasing sensitivity to yen weakness. Summary: Japan’s FX chief Atsushi Mimura warns authorities are ready to take “decisive” action if speculative moves...

articles.title: BoJ’s Ueda: FX moves are among factors that have huge impact on Japan's economy, prices
articles.expand: Bank of Japan (BoJ) Governor Kazuo Ueda said during Asian trading hours on Monday, after the release of the central bank’s Summary of Opinions, that changes in the foreign exchange (FX) market are key factors that have a huge impact on Japan's economy, and prices.

articles.title: Bank of Japan Governor Ueda says will closely watch FX moves (USD/JPY drops)
articles.expand: BoJ Governor Ueda emphasised the growing impact of FX on inflation and policy, reinforcing that currency moves are now a key input into decision-making. While not verbal intervention, the comments supported the yen by signalling increased sensitivity to FX-driven inflation. Summary: Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda highlighted FX as a key driver o...

articles.title: Banks ask RBI for 3 months to comply with FX position caps: Sources
articles.expand: Indian banks urge RBI for a three-month extension on new foreign exchange position limits to avoid forced unwinding of NDF trades, amid rupee pressure from oil price surge and Iran war impact.

articles.title: ECB Schnabel: There is no need to rush into action
articles.expand: ECB's Schnabel is speaking and says: We are facing a massive energy price shock. There is no need to rush into action. We have time to analyze the data. ECB should not be rushed to raise rates. This has a stagflation-lite feel—the energy shock keeps inflation risks alive, but the wait-and-see stance tells you the ECB isn’t in a hurry to support gro...

articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: War headlines and US employment to set the tone
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair ends the week around 1.1530, barely below its opening but off the peak at 1.1640. Widespread optimism on Monday was diluted as days went by, and financial markets moved once again on war-related headlines.
